item.title: When traffic stops in Germany, drivers are required by law to move to the shoulder of the road on both sides to create an open lane for emergency vehicles. This is called traffic education, and it should be universal.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: Michael John Berryman.
item.description: Michael John Berryman, born on September 4, 1948, in Los Angeles, California, has carved out a distinctive niche in the film industry with his unique physical appearance and compelling performances. His journey from a challenging childhood to becoming a beloved character actor in cult and horror films is a testament to his resilience and talent. Michael Berryman was the second of two children born to Sloan and Barbara Berryman. His father was a U.S. Navy neurosurgeon who was deployed to Hiroshima in the aftermath of the atomic bombing, and his mother was a nurse. Michael was born with hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia, a rare genetic condition that affects the development of sweat glands, skin, hair, and fingernails. This condition also led to cranial synostosis, necessitating cranial reconstructive surgery during his childhood. Despite these challenges, Berryman had a supportive family. He was raised Roman Catholic and attended Saint Monica Preparatory in Santa Monica. His early interests included surfing, which he took up with enthusiasm despite his physical limitations. After completing high school, he pursued studies in botany and zoology at California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo. Before entering the acting world, Berryman worked as a florist, a job that showcased his attention to detail and appreciation for nature. Berryman's unusual appearance due to his medical condition unexpectedly opened doors in Hollywood. He gained early recognition for his role in Miloš Forman's 'One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest' (1975), where he played Ellis. This Academy Award-winning film provided a significant boost to his career, leading to further opportunities in the horror genre. In 1977, Berryman starred as Pluto in Wes Craven's cult classic 'The Hills Have Eyes'. The film's success cemented his reputation as a distinctive character actor. He reprised this role in the sequel, 'The Hills Have Eyes Part II' (1985), despite the challenging filming conditions in the desert heat, which required him to take special precautions due to his lack of sweat glands. Berryman continued to make a mark in various other films and TV shows. His filmography includes roles in 'Weird Science' (1985), where he played a mutant biker, and 'The Guyver' (1991), portraying the character Lisker. He also appeared in the science fiction and fantasy genres, with roles in 'Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home' (1986) and 'The Crow' (1994), although his scenes in the latter were cut from the theatrical release. Berryman's career is punctuated by a variety of memorable roles in both film and television. He appeared in 'Star Trek: The Next Generation' as Captain Rixx and had guest roles in popular TV series like 'The X-Files' and 'Highway to Heaven', where he notably portrayed Satan in two episodes. His versatility extended to music videos as well. Berryman featured in Mötley Crüe's video for "Smokin' In the Boys Room" and the introduction to their song "Home Sweet Home". These appearances further solidified his status as a cultural icon in the horror and cult film community. In the 2000s, Berryman continued to engage with his fans by attending numerous genre conventions, including the Horrorfind convention and the Eerie Horror Film Festival. He starred in the British-Canadian horror film 'Below Zero' (2012) and made appearances in various other films like 'The Devil's Rejects' (2005) and 'Smothered' (2016). Beyond his acting career, Berryman is a passionate advocate for environmental protection. He lived on a wolf sanctuary for ten years, demonstrating his commitment to wildlife conservation. His personal experiences and advocacy work have endeared him to fans beyond his on-screen persona. Michael Berryman's career is a remarkable example of how an individual can turn personal challenges into professional strengths. His distinctive appearance and talent have made him a beloved figure in the horror and cult film genres. From 'One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest' (1975) to 'The Hills Have Eyes' (1977), and his enduring presence in various conventions, Berryman's influence on the genre is undeniable. In recent years, Berryman continued to work in the film industry, starring in the horror film 'They Turned Us Into Killers' (2024). His career spans several decades, showcasing a diverse range of characters that have left a lasting impression on audiences. Michael Berryman's story is one of resilience, talent, and an unyielding spirit. His contributions to the film industry, particularly in horror and cult classics, have earned him a place in the hearts of fans worldwide. His journey from a young boy with a rare genetic condition to a celebrated actor is truly inspirational, proving that uniqueness can be a powerful asset in the world of entertainment.

item.title: Kami Rita Sherpa,a Legendary Sherpa, was born on 17th January, 1970 in Thame Village, Solu Khumbu Region of Nepal. Thame is also the village of Legendary Tenzing Norgay. He has reached the summit of Mt.Everest 30 times.
item.description: Kami Rita Sherpa, a Legendary Sherpa, was born on 17th January, 1970 in Thame Village, Solu Khumbu Region of Nepal. Thame is also the village of Legendary Tenzing Norgay. He has reached the summit of Mt.Everest 30 times. Kami Rita has been a guide for more than two decades now who had first summited Everest in 1994 during a commercial expedition. During these 29 years, he has scaled five 8,000-metre peaks, summiting them 44 times. He has also scaled the world’s second-highest mountain, K-2, in Pakistan. Kami Rita Sherpa is a Nepali Sherpa guide who, since May 2023 has held the record for most ascents to the summit of Mount Everest. He scaled the mountain for 30th time (twice) in May 2023, eclipsing his record set on 17th May, 2023. His father was among the first professional Sherpa guides after Everest was opened to foreign mountaineers in 1950. His brother, Lakpa Rita Sherpa, also a guide, scaled Everest 17 times.“I can climb for a few more years,” said Kami Rita Sherpa some time ago in a BBC interview. “I am healthy. I can go until I am 60 years old. With oxygen it’s no big deal, we were born in the Himalayas.” Once Kami Rita told the BBC. “I never thought about making records. I actually didn’t know that you could make a record. Had I known, I would have done a lot more summits earlier.” Kami Rita has retained his humility towards Mount Everest. For the Sherpas, Chomolungma, as they call it, is a sacred mountain on which a goddess lives. “Months before I begin an ascent, I start worshiping and asking for her forgiveness because I have to put my feet on her body. Right before the final steps to the summit, others run to take photos but I bow my head again and ask for her forgiveness,” says Kami Rita Sherpa. “No matter how strong you are, how good you prepare, you have to be blessed by God to reach the top. In addition to his Everest completions, his total includes Cho-Oyu eight times, three times on Manaslu, and both Lhotse and K2 once each. Watch out for his ascents on Everest 1994 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 13th May, via SE Ridge 1995 - Had to abandon summit push at 8500 M 1997 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 25th May,1997 through SE Ridge 1998 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 25th May, 1998 through SE Ridge 1999 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 13th May, 1999 through SE Ridge 2000 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 23rd May, 2000 through SE Ridge 2002 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest via S Col - SE Ridge 2003 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 30th May, 2003 through SE Ridge 2004 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 24th May, 2004 through SE Ridge 2005 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 30th May, 2005 through SE Ridge 2006 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 20th May, 2006 through SE Ridge 2007 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 22nd May, 2007 through SE Ridge 2008 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 24th May, 2008 through SE Ridge 2009 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 23rd May, 2009 through SE Ridge 2010 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 5th May, 2010 through SE Ridge 2012 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 18th May, 2012 through SE Ridge 2013 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 22nd May, 2013 through SE Ridge 2015 - All expeditions were called-off due to Earthquake 2016 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 20th May, 2016 through NE Ridge 2017 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 27th May, 2017 through SE Ridge 2018 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 16th May, 2018 through SE Ridge 2019 - Successfully reached the summit of Mt.Everest on 15th May, 2019 through SE Ridge 2019 : Successfully reached the summit of Everest for second time 2021 : Successfully reached the summit of Everest through SE Ridge(25 times in totality) within a week of Mt.Everest on 21st May, 2019 2022 : 26th Summit of Everest through SE Ridge 2023 : 17th May, 2023 - 27th Summit of Everest through SE Ridge 2023 : 23rd May, 2023 - 28th summit through SE Ridge May, 2024 - 29th Summit through SE Ridge May, 2024 - 30th summit through SE Ridge Pic Credit - Instagram of Kami Rita Sherpa

item.title: What Happened Today #80
item.description: They protect each other and lie about us. From billionaires buying appointments to military strike plans dropped in family group chats, today’s leaders aren’t defending America. They’re shielding themselves and calling it patriotism. Meanwhile, the leader of the nation's health infrastructure is spreading lies about people with autism, backtracking, and trying to pin the condition on "environmental toxins" that he says will yield payments for those with severe autism. After a weekend of bold protests, even Congress is flinching — some are finally admitting Trump has (gasp) made mistakes. Keep calling. Keep writing. Keep showing up. Keep reminding them. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-family-chat 2. https://go.govbrief.today/yemen-monday 3. https://go.govbrief.today/salvador-venezuela 4. https://go.govbrief.today/documents-uploaded 5. https://go.govbrief.today/pope-vance 6. https://go.govbrief.today/fundraising-inaurguration 7. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-escalation 8. https://go.govbrief.today/rfk-autism 9. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-easter 10. https://go.govbrief.today/may-day-protest

item.title: They are hearing me!!
item.description: My foster kittens have been so young that they haven’t responded to my voice until the last couple of days. Tonight was the biggest indication that they can hear me and are responding! Mama Purrsephone is gonna get an extra can of wet food tonight. This is so exciting!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
